title: STAR WARS Battlefront II

notes:
No matter what Proton version I try, the game just crashes. 
After some tinkering (removing the game's folder from compatdata) it launched, and I actually got into a game, but the game kept crashing after like 10-30 minutes of gameplay.
Then using the newest GE Proton I managed to run the game, and actually play for more than an hour.
Then the next day the game just kept crashing every time I joined a game, again no matter what proton version I used.
On windows the game works even when started from an EXT4 drive, so I suppose there is something wrong either with my linux installation, or the game itself is just borked on linux for now.
title: STAR WARS Battlefront II

notes: Works pretty flawlessly and runs really well

title: STAR WARS Battlefront II

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (navi21, LLVM 15.0.3, DRM 3.49, 5.15.0-56-generic)
notes:
It has run several times in the last weeks. The game started mostly but not allways. Repeating the start did it mostly. When it has run, it was smoothly enought to play, even multiplayer battles.

But a few days ago it didn't anymore. I tried different proton versions and ge-proton versions. The startup is pretty pretty slow, thanks to the shitty EA App. This App kills the game for me almost. It is so useless.
Nevermind, the launcher starts an game windows comes up for a second. There is a error message that is closed instantly, so you can't read it. I recorded my screen to make it visible. The message says: "Wine C++ Runtime Library, Assertion failed!, Expression: "!status".

I've also tried to start the EA App manually, so it can start up and connect before starting the game. But this didn't worked as well. Deleting the app-id folder in steamapps/compdata didn't work also.

So actually this game is broken for my setup. It's just so frustrating. :(
title: STAR WARS Battlefront II

notes: Perfectly Playable, make sure to install EA app
